title: Total RNA Extraction, Quantification and Illumina Library Preparation
author: Antonio Mondini
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.457gy9n

steps.title: Once completed the filtration, transfer the...
steps.instruction: Once completed the filtration, transfer the filters in the 15 mL tubes with beads (provided in the RNeasy®kit). Transfer the filters carefully folding it to fit in the 15mL tubes using two forceps and letting the surface with visible filtered particles inward the tube. Push carefully down to the beads. No more than 2 filters can be added to a single tube

steps.title: Add G2 and snap freeze immersing...
steps.instruction: Add G2 and snap freeze immersing the bead tubes 15 seconds in liquid nitrogen keeping the lid slightly open Add 500µLof G2 each filter. If a thick sediment layer accumulates on the filter, it has to be weighed and 500 µL of G2 added each 0.25 grams of sediments. No more than 1 gram can be added to the tube. Addition of more soil/sediments can reduce the lyse cells efficiency. For more info on G2 test see appendix 1 go to step #133 . Alternatively, to liquid nitrogen, tubes can be freeze O/N at -20°C

steps.title: Immediately add 2
steps.instruction: Immediately add 2.5 ml of PowerBead Solution

steps.title: Add 0
steps.instruction: Add 0.25 ml of Solution SR1

steps.title: Add 0
steps.instruction: Add 0.8 ml of Solution IRS

steps.title: Add 3
steps.instruction: Add 3.5 ml of phenol/chloroform/isoamyl alcohol (pH 6.5) and vortex until the solution is homogeneous. After vortexing the solution should have a muddy consistency. At this stage the tubes have to be placed outside the Microbiological hood and to avoid phenol chloroform diffusion in the air is preferably to seal the tubes using parafilm

steps.title: Place the PowerBead tube horizontally on a Vortex Adapter (cat
steps.instruction: Place the PowerBead tube horizontally on a Vortex Adapter (cat. no. 13000-V1-15) and vortex at maximum speed for 20 minutes at Room temperature This vortexing time was specifically adapted for tubes containing filters with the addition of Phenil chloroform and previously snap freezed in Liquid Nitrogen. For different samples vortex for 15 minutes according to the manufacturer protocol

steps.title: Remove PowerBead tube from the shaker...
steps.instruction: Remove PowerBead tube from the shaker and centrifuge Room temperature for 10 minutes at 2,500 x g For different centrifuge unit see appendix 2 go to step #134

steps.title: Transfer the upper aqueous phase (avoid...
steps.instruction: Transfer the upper aqueous phase (avoid the interphase and lower phenol layer) to a clean 15 ml Collection Tube (provided) using a 1000µL pipette. Ensure to take only the top layer (Approx. 3 mL) leaving some aqueous phase to not disturb the interphase. If the filters, after centrifuge, are interfering with normal procedure of pipetting or have formed agglomerates, pour the tube content (leaving behind the filters) in a new 15 mL tube and repeat the centrifugation at step 9.

steps.title: Add 1
steps.instruction: Add 1.5 ml of Solution SR3 to the tube containing the aqueous phase and vortex to mix until the solution gets homogeneous

steps.title: Incubate at 4 °C for 10 minutes
steps.instruction: Incubate at 4 °C for 10 minutes

steps.title: Centrifuge at 2,500 x g Room temperature for 10 minutes
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 2,500 x g Room temperature for 10 minutes

steps.title: Transfer the supernatant, without disturbing the...
steps.instruction: Transfer the supernatant, without disturbing the pellet if present (usually not present), to a new 15 ml Collection Tube (provided) Its preferred to leave behind few microliters

steps.title: Add 5 mL of Solution SR4...
steps.instruction: Add 5 mL of Solution SR4 to the supernatant in the Collection Tube using a serological 5 mL pipette

steps.title: Vortex until the biphasic layer disappear...
steps.instruction: Vortex until the biphasic layer disappear (2 cycles of 10 seconds)

steps.title: Incubate at Room temperature for 30 minutes
steps.instruction: Incubate at Room temperature for 30 minutes

steps.title: Centrifuge at 2500 x g at...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 2500 x g at Room temperature for 30 minutes

steps.title: Decant the supernatant
steps.instruction: Decant the supernatant

steps.title: Quickly spin down the tubes to...
steps.instruction: Quickly spin down the tubes to collect remaining drops on the tubes wall

steps.title: Remove the collected liquid without disturbing the pellet
steps.instruction: Remove the collected liquid without disturbing the pellet

steps.title: Allow the tubes to dry for...
steps.instruction: Allow the tubes to dry for 5 minutes Do not let the pellet over dry (more than 10 minutes) or will result hard to resuspend it. Pellet colors were observed in a range of light grey and more often light/dark brown.

steps.title: Shake Solution SR5 and add 1...
steps.instruction: Shake Solution SR5 and add 1 ml to the 15 ml Collection Tube.

steps.title: Resuspend the pellet completely by repeatedly pipetting
steps.instruction: Resuspend the pellet completely by repeatedly pipetting. Usually is hard to be resuspended and using a P1000 pipette just remove the pellet from the wall and proceed to the next step.

steps.title: Incubate the tubes at 45 °C in a preheated thermoblock
steps.instruction: Incubate the tubes at 45 °C in a preheated thermoblock. Check on the tube every 5 min and vortex the solution. Visual inspection should reveal the total resuspension.

steps.title: Repeat step 25 until the pellet is fully resuspended
steps.instruction: Repeat step 25 until the pellet is fully resuspended

steps.title: Once the pellet is resuspended place...
steps.instruction: Once the pellet is resuspended place the JetStar Mini Column (provided capture column) in a new 15 ml tube

steps.title: Shake SR5 solution and add 2...
steps.instruction: Shake SR5 solution and add 2 mL to the RNA capture column From this step on never allow to completely gravity flow through the column.A dry column will reduce the downstream RNA yield.Add warning here

steps.title: Once the SR5 solution has reached...
steps.instruction: Once the SR5 solution has reached the top of the resin (1 mm thickness) in the column add the dissolved pellet to the column and allow it to gravity flow through the column

steps.title: When dissolved pellet has reached the...
steps.instruction: When dissolved pellet has reached the top of the resin (1 mm thickness) add 1 ml of shacked SR5 solution to the capture column and allow it to gravity flow through it

steps.title: Once the SR5 solution has reached...
steps.instruction: Once the SR5 solution has reached the top of the resin shake solution SR6 and add 1 mL to the column

steps.title: Immediately transfer the RNA capture column...
steps.instruction: Immediately transfer the RNA capture column to a new clean 15 mL tube

steps.title: Allow solution SR6 to flow through the column
steps.instruction: Allow solution SR6 to flow through the column. Here wait until all the column is completely dry

steps.title: Transfer the eluted RNA to a 2
steps.instruction: Transfer the eluted RNA to a 2.2 mL Collection Tube (provided)

steps.title: Shake solution SR4 and add 1mL to the 2
steps.instruction: Shake solution SR4 and add 1mL to the 2.2 mL collection tube. Close gently the lid and invert tube to mix 10 times.

steps.title: Incubate tube at -20 °C for 30 minutes
steps.instruction: Incubate tube at -20 °C for 30 minutes

steps.title: Centrifuge the 2
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the 2.2 vials at 4 °C for 15 minutes at 13,000 x g After centrifugation the pellet may have a light brown color or white. If the final RNA yield is low may be barely visible and in this case is recommendable to place the vials in the centrifuge memorizing the position where the centrifugation force may deposit the pellet in order to have a proper final RNA resuspension.

steps.title: Decant the supernatant
steps.instruction: Decant the supernatant

steps.title: Quickly spin down the tubes to...
steps.instruction: Quickly spin down the tubes to collect remaining drops on the tubes wall

steps.title: Remove the supernatant using a smaller...
steps.instruction: Remove the supernatant using a smaller pipette (P20 or P100) without touching the pellet

steps.title: Air dry the pellet for 5 minutes
steps.instruction: Air dry the pellet for 5 minutes

steps.title: Resuspend the RNA pellet in 40 µL of Solution SR7
steps.instruction: Resuspend the RNA pellet in 40 µL of Solution SR7

steps.title: Split the resuspended RNA in clean...
steps.instruction: Split the resuspended RNA in clean RNase free 200µL PCR tubes as follows. Final Volume (FV) 40 µL Tube 1 – 17 µL Tube 2 – 17 µL Tube 3 – ~ 6 µL Tube 3 will be used for RNA quantification and RIN assessment while tube number 1 will be used for the downstream DNase treatment. Tube 2 can be stored at -80°C as a back-up sample in case of problems during the downstream treatments. If a low RNA yield is occurring (according to preliminary tests) resuspend the RNA pellet (step 42) in a 20 µL final volume and split the sample as follows: Tube 1 – 17 µL Tube 2 – ~3 µL

steps.title: Place the PCR tubes on ice...
steps.instruction: Place the PCR tubes on ice and proceed to step 45 or place the samples at -20 °C for short storage and -80 °C for longer

steps.title: Place the PCR tubes containing the eluted RNA on ice
steps.instruction: Place the PCR tubes containing the eluted RNA on ice

steps.title: Determine concentration of RNA using Qubit
steps.instruction: Determine concentration of RNA using Qubit. Take 2-4 µL from tube #3

steps.title: Thaw on ice the tube #1...
steps.instruction: Thaw on ice the tube #1 containing 17µL total RNA extraction from step 43 go to step #43

steps.title: Add 2µL of 10X DNase Buffer
steps.instruction: Add 2µL of 10X DNase Buffer

steps.title: Add 1 µL of DNase I enzyme (10 units)
steps.instruction: Add 1 µL of DNase I enzyme (10 units)

steps.title: Mix by gently pipetting Don’t vortex...
steps.instruction: Mix by gently pipetting Don’t vortex the DNase I enzyme or the final mixture. It will denature the enzyme

steps.title: Incubate the mixture, using a thermocycler,...
steps.instruction: Incubate the mixture, using a thermocycler, at 37 °C for 20 minutes and proceed to the next step, the DNase removal

steps.title: Resuspend the DNase Removal resin by...
steps.instruction: Resuspend the DNase Removal resin by vortexing until the slurry is homogeneous

steps.title: Add 5 µL of DNase removal...
steps.instruction: Add 5 µL of DNase removal resin to the mixture (step 51) go to step #51

steps.title: Incubate for 10 minutes at Room...
steps.instruction: Incubate for 10 minutes at Room temperature and check on the tube every 2 minutes with resuspending the resin by gently pipetting the solution

steps.title: Centrifuge the tubes at 13
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the tubes at 13.000 x g for 1 minute to pellet the resin

steps.title: Transfer the supernatant containing the RNA...
steps.instruction: Transfer the supernatant containing the RNA target to a new clean 200µL tube without touching or removing the resin To avoid contaminant carryover pipette approximately 15µL

steps.title: Determine the successful DNA removal procedure...
steps.instruction: Determine the successful DNA removal procedure using Qubit or for higher detection limit perform a qPCR control. Use 1 to 5 µL from the previous step (56)

steps.title: Determine concentration of RNA using Qubit
steps.instruction: Determine concentration of RNA using Qubit. Use 1-2 µL

steps.title: Proceed to the RIN assessment number...
steps.instruction: Proceed to the RIN assessment number or store the RNA at -80 °C

steps.title: Allow all reagents to equilibrate to...
steps.instruction: Allow all reagents to equilibrate to room temperature for 30 minutes before use and protect dye and dye mixture from light.

steps.title: Set up the priming station for...
steps.instruction: Set up the priming station for Bioanalyzer® chip adjusting the base plate in C position and sliding the syringe clip up to the top position Be sure of the clip position. Different position will lead to a negative output

steps.title: Prepare the RNA ladder and the...
steps.instruction: Prepare the RNA ladder and the gel Ladder and gel preparation described in appendix 3 go to step #135

steps.title: Vortex RNA dye concentrate (blue cap...
steps.instruction: Vortex RNA dye concentrate (blue cap vial) for 10 seconds, spin down and add 1 μL of dye into a 65 μL aliquot of filtered gel

steps.title: Vortex solution well
steps.instruction: Vortex solution well. Spin tube at 13000g for 10 minutes at Room temperature Use prepared gel-dye mix within one day

steps.title: Place a new RNA chip on...
steps.instruction: Place a new RNA chip on the chip priming station Always insert the pipette tip to the bottom of the well when dispensing the liquid. Placing the pipette at the edge of the well may lead to poor results.

steps.title: Pipette 9 μL of gel-dye mix in the well marked
steps.instruction: Pipette 9 μL of gel-dye mix in the well marked

steps.title: Make sure that the plunger is...
steps.instruction: Make sure that the plunger is positioned at 1 mL and then close the chip priming station

steps.title: Press plunger until it is held by the clip
steps.instruction: Press plunger until it is held by the clip

steps.title: Wait for exactly 30 seconds then release clip
steps.instruction: Wait for exactly 30 seconds then release clip

steps.title: Wait for 5 seconds and slowly...
steps.instruction: Wait for 5 seconds and slowly pull back plunger to 1 mL position

steps.title: Open the chip priming station and...
steps.instruction: Open the chip priming station and pipette 9 μL of gel-dye mix in the wells marked with

steps.title: Pipette 9 μL of the RNA...
steps.instruction: Pipette 9 μL of the RNA conditioning solution (vial with white cap) into the well marked CS

steps.title: Pipette 5 μL of RNA marker...
steps.instruction: Pipette 5 μL of RNA marker (green cap vial ) in all 11 sample wells and in the well marked

steps.title: Pipette 1 μL of the heat...
steps.instruction: Pipette 1 μL of the heat denatured and aliquoted ladder in the well marked

steps.title: Pipette 1 μL of sample in...
steps.instruction: Pipette 1 μL of sample in each of the 11 sample wells. Pipette 1 μL of RNA Marker (green cap vial) in each unused sample well It is recommended to heat denature all RNA samples before use for 2 min and 70 °C (once) and keep them on ice

steps.title: Place the chip horizontally in the...
steps.instruction: Place the chip horizontally in the IKA vortexer and vortex for 1 min at 2400rpm. DO NOT VORTEX FOR MORE THAN 1 MINUTE AND MORE THAN 2400rpm

steps.title: Place the chip in the Agilent...
steps.instruction: Place the chip in the Agilent 2100 Bioanalyzer and run the instrument within 5 min Set up the Bioanalyzer instrument prior loading the chip. Keeping loaded gel for more than 5 minutes will allow the liquid in it to evaporate leading to a poor or non-acceptable analysis. For a clear overview of the RNA integrity electropherogram output from the bioanalyzer see appendix 4 go to step #137

steps.title: This procedure was used for libraries...
steps.instruction: This procedure was used for libraries without any enrichment or depletion of total RNA with RIN scores > 7 and a concentration of 100 ng purified RNA. RNA was prepared in 5 μL final volume of Nuclease-free water, to start with the first protocol step. The protocol is optimized for approximately 200 nt RNA inserts. To generate libraries with longer RNA insert sizes or lower RIN number, refer to Neb manufacturer protocol. RNA Fragmentation and Priming Assemble all the reaction on ice using a nuclease-free tubes and in a dedicated clean PCR hood. The Illumina library preparation requires 7 steps using the thermocycler with different incubation times/cycles and temperatures; it is highly recommended to create a dedicated folder in the thermocycler with saved programs as suggested in the protocol in a chronological way. See appendix 5 go to step #138 to set up the thermocycler Thaw the RNA samples from step 58 on ice go to step #58

steps.title: Perform dilutions, if necessary, to obtain...
steps.instruction: Perform dilutions, if necessary, to obtain a 100ng of total RNA in 5µL Final Volume using new clean 200µL PCR tubes

steps.title: Set up the reaction (using the...
steps.instruction: Set up the reaction (using the tubes from the previous step) as follows:

steps.title: Set a 20 μL pipette to...
steps.instruction: Set a 20 μL pipette to 5 μL and then pipette the entire volume up and down at least 10 times to mix thoroughly. Perform a quick spin to collect all liquid from the sides of the tube

steps.title: Place the sample in a thermocycler,...
steps.instruction: Place the sample in a thermocycler, upload and start program #1 For program #1 see appendix 5 go to step #138

steps.title: Once the incubation is ended, immediately...
steps.instruction: Once the incubation is ended, immediately transfer the tube to ice

steps.title: Keeping the tubes on ice, assemble the following reaction:
steps.instruction: Keeping the tubes on ice, assemble the following reaction:

steps.title: Set a 20 μL pipette to...
steps.instruction: Set a 20 μL pipette to 10 μL and then pipette the entire volume up and down at least 10 times to mix thoroughly. Perform a quick spin to collect all liquid from the sides of the tube

steps.title: Place the sample in a thermocycler,...
steps.instruction: Place the sample in a thermocycler, upload and start program #2 For program #2 see appendix 5 go to step #138

steps.title: Once the incubation is ended, immediately...
steps.instruction: Once the incubation is ended, immediately transfer the tube to ice and proceed to next step

steps.title: Keeping the tubes on ice, assemble the following reaction:
steps.instruction: Keeping the tubes on ice, assemble the following reaction:

steps.title: Set a 100 μL pipette to...
steps.instruction: Set a 100 μL pipette to 40 μL and then pipette the entire volume up and down at least 10 times to mix thoroughly. Perform a quick spin to collect all liquid from the sides of the tube

steps.title: Place the sample in a thermocycler,...
steps.instruction: Place the sample in a thermocycler, upload and start program #3 For program #3 see appendix 5 go to step #138

steps.title: Once the incubation is ended proceed...
steps.instruction: Once the incubation is ended proceed to the first Purification step (91.1)

steps.title: Before starting, prepare 80% ethanol; use...
steps.instruction: Before starting, prepare 80% ethanol; use a daily freshly prepared one. It is preferably to process no more than 6 samples at once; this will reduce the risk of over-dry the beads with a lower DNA target recovery. In this protocol the purification was performed usingNEBNext Sample Purification Beads Resuspend the NEBNext Sample Purification Beads by vortexing until the slurry is homogeneous

steps.title: Add 144 μL (1
steps.instruction: Add 144 μL (1.8X) of resuspended beads to the tubes from step 89 (second strand synthesis, ~80 μl) go to step #89

steps.title: Mix well on a vortex mixer
steps.instruction: Mix well on a vortex mixer

steps.title: Incubate for 5 minutes at Room temperature
steps.instruction: Incubate for 5 minutes at Room temperature

steps.title: Briefly spin the tube in a...
steps.instruction: Briefly spin the tube in a microcentrifuge to collect any sample on the sides of the tube

steps.title: Place the tube on a magnetic...
steps.instruction: Place the tube on a magnetic rack for ~5 minutes to separate beads from the supernatant

steps.title: Once the solution is clear, carefully...
steps.instruction: Once the solution is clear, carefully remove and discard the supernatant without either removing or touching the beads Be careful with the beads, they retain the target DNA

steps.title: Add 200 μL of 80% ethanol...
steps.instruction: Add 200 μL of 80% ethanol to the tube while in the magnetic rack

steps.title: Incubate at Room temperature for 30...
steps.instruction: Incubate at Room temperature for 30 seconds and then carefully remove and discard the supernatant

steps.title: Repeat from Step 91
steps.instruction: Repeat from Step 91.8 once for a total of 2 washing steps

steps.title: Briefly spin the tubes and put...
steps.instruction: Briefly spin the tubes and put the tubes back on the magnetic rack

steps.title: Remove the residual ethanol pipetting and...
steps.instruction: Remove the residual ethanol pipetting and air dry the beads for up to 5 minutes while the tube is on the magnetic rack with lid open Do not over-dry the beads. Elute the samples when the beads are still dark brown and glossy looking, but when all visible liquid has evaporated. When the beads turn lighter brown and start to crack they are too dry

steps.title: Remove the tube from the magnetic rack
steps.instruction: Remove the tube from the magnetic rack

steps.title: Add 53μL of 0
steps.instruction: Add 53μL of 0.1X TE Buffer (provided) to elute the DNA from the beads

steps.title: Mix well on a vortex mixer
steps.instruction: Mix well on a vortex mixer

steps.title: Quickly spin the tube and incubate...
steps.instruction: Quickly spin the tube and incubate for 2 minutes at Room temperature

steps.title: Place the tube on the magnetic...
steps.instruction: Place the tube on the magnetic rack for ~5 minutes, until the solution is clear

steps.title: Remove 50μL of the supernatant and...
steps.instruction: Remove 50μL of the supernatant and transfer to a clean 200µL nuclease free PCR tube without disturbing the beads pellet If you need to stop, this is a safe stop point. Samples can be stored at -20°C

steps.title: Place the tube from step 91
steps.instruction: Place the tube from step 91.18 on ice and assemble the following reaction: Here is possible to prepare a mastermix. If a master mix is made, add 10μL of master mix to 50μL of cDNA for the End Prep reaction

steps.title: Set a 100 μL pipette to...
steps.instruction: Set a 100 μL pipette to 50 μL and then pipette the entire volume up and down at least 10 times to mix thoroughly. Perform a quick spin to collect all liquid from the sides of the tube It is important to mix well. The presence of a small amount of bubbles will not interfere

steps.title: Place the sample in a thermocycler,...
steps.instruction: Place the sample in a thermocycler, upload and start program #4. Immediately proceed to the next step For program #4 see appendix 5 go to step #138

steps.title: During the incubation, perform a 5-fold...
steps.instruction: During the incubation, perform a 5-fold dilution of the (red) NEBNext Adaptor in Adaptor Dilution Buffer and keep the diluted adaptors on ice The NEBNext adaptor is provided in NEBNext oligos

steps.title: Once both the Adaptor dilution and...
steps.instruction: Once both the Adaptor dilution and incubation are done, assemble the ligation reaction on ice by adding the following components Follow the order given. We do not recommend a premix

steps.title: Set a 100 μL pipette to...
steps.instruction: Set a 100 μL pipette to 80 μL and then pipette the entire volume up and down at least 10 times to mix thoroughly. Perform a quick spin to collect all liquid from the sides of the tube The NEBNext Ultra II Ligation Master Mix is very viscous. Care should be taken to ensure adequate mixing of the ligation reaction, as incomplete mixing will result in reduced ligation efficiency. The presence of a small amount of bubbles will not interfere with performance

steps.title: Place the sample in a thermocycler,...
steps.instruction: Place the sample in a thermocycler, upload and start program #5 For program #5 see appendix 5 go to step #138

steps.title: Add 3 μL (blue cap vial)...
steps.instruction: Add 3 μL (blue cap vial) USER Enzyme to the ligation mixture after the incubation, resulting in total volume of 96.5 μL

steps.title: Set a 100 μL pipette to...
steps.instruction: Set a 100 μL pipette to 80 μL and then pipette the entire volume up and down at least 10 times to mix thoroughly. Perform a quick spin to collect all liquid from the sides of the tube

steps.title: Place the sample in a thermocycler,...
steps.instruction: Place the sample in a thermocycler, upload and start program #6 For program #6 see appendix 5 go to step #138

steps.title: Once the incubation is ended proceed...
steps.instruction: Once the incubation is ended proceed to the second Purification step

steps.title: Resuspend the NEBNext Sample Purification Beads...
steps.instruction: Resuspend the NEBNext Sample Purification Beads by vortexing until the slurry is homogeneous

steps.title: Add 87μL (0
steps.instruction: Add 87μL (0.9X) of resuspended beads to the tubes from step 100 (Ligation reaction, ~96.5μL)

steps.title: Mix well on a vortex mixer
steps.instruction: Mix well on a vortex mixer

steps.title: Incubate for 10 minutes at Room temperature
steps.instruction: Incubate for 10 minutes at Room temperature

steps.title: Briefly spin the tube in a...
steps.instruction: Briefly spin the tube in a microcentrifuge to collect any sample on the sides of the tube

steps.title: Place the tube on a magnetic...
steps.instruction: Place the tube on a magnetic rack for ~5 minutes to separate beads from the supernatant

steps.title: Once the solution is clear, carefully...
steps.instruction: Once the solution is clear, carefully remove and discard the supernatant without either removing or touching the beads Be careful with the beads, they retain the target DNA

steps.title: Add 200 μL of 80% ethanol...
steps.instruction: Add 200 μL of 80% ethanol to the tube while in the magnetic rack

steps.title: Incubate at room temperature for 30...
steps.instruction: Incubate at room temperature for 30 seconds, and then carefully remove and discard the supernatant

steps.title: Repeat from Step 102
steps.instruction: Repeat from Step 102.8 once for a total of 2 washing steps

steps.title: Briefly spin the tubes and put...
steps.instruction: Briefly spin the tubes and put the tubes back on the magnetic rack

steps.title: Remove the residual ethanol pipetting and...
steps.instruction: Remove the residual ethanol pipetting and air dry the beads for up to 5 minutes while the tube is on the magnetic rack with lid open Do not over-dry the beads. Elute the samples when the beads are still dark brown and glossy looking, but when all visible liquid has evaporated. When the beads turn lighter brown and start to crack they are too dry

steps.title: Remove the tube from the magnetic rack
steps.instruction: Remove the tube from the magnetic rack

steps.title: Add 17μL of of 0
steps.instruction: Add 17μL of of 0.1X TE Buffer (provided) to elute the DNA from the beads

steps.title: Mix well on a vortex mixer
steps.instruction: Mix well on a vortex mixer

steps.title: Quickly spin the tube and incubate...
steps.instruction: Quickly spin the tube and incubate for 2 minutes at Room temperature

steps.title: Place the tube on the magnetic...
steps.instruction: Place the tube on the magnetic rack for ~5 minutes, until the solution is clear

steps.title: Remove 15μL of the supernatant and...
steps.instruction: Remove 15μL of the supernatant and transfer to a clean 200µL nuclease free PCR tube without disturbing the beads pellet If you need to stop, this is a safe stop point. Samples can be stored at -20°C

steps.title: Place the tube from step 102
steps.instruction: Place the tube from step 102.18 on ice and assemble the following reaction: The following PCR Enrichment was performed specifically using NEBNext Multiplex Oligos for Illumina (Index Primers set 1) (NEB #E7335S) with the oligos concentration of 10 µM. The PCR cycles are recommended using an initial RNA concentration of 100 ng; limit the PCR cycles to avoid overamplification with a secondary unwanted peak (~1000 bp) which will appear on the Bioanalyzer The Universal PCR Primer should be added to all the samples while the indexing should be unique for each sample. See appendix 6 go to step #139 for an example of assembling reaction

steps.title: Place the sample in a thermocycler,...
steps.instruction: Place the sample in a thermocycler, upload and start program #7 For program #7 see appendix 5 go to step #138

steps.title: Once the PCR amplification is ended...
steps.instruction: Once the PCR amplification is ended proceed to the last Purification step

steps.title: Resuspend the NEBNext Sample Purification Beads...
steps.instruction: Resuspend the NEBNext Sample Purification Beads by vortexing until the slurry is homogeneous

steps.title: Add 45μL (0
steps.instruction: Add 45μL (0.9X) of resuspended beads to the tubes from step 100 (PCR reaction, ~50μL)

steps.title: Incubate for 5 minutes at Room temperature
steps.instruction: Incubate for 5 minutes at Room temperature

steps.title: Mix well on a vortex mixer
steps.instruction: Mix well on a vortex mixer

steps.title: Briefly spin the tube in a...
steps.instruction: Briefly spin the tube in a microcentrifuge to collect any sample on the sides of the tube

steps.title: Place the tube on a magnetic...
steps.instruction: Place the tube on a magnetic rack for ~5 minutes to separate beads from the supernatant

steps.title: Once the solution is clear, carefully...
steps.instruction: Once the solution is clear, carefully remove and discard the supernatant without either removing or touching the beads Be careful with the beads, they retain the target DNA

steps.title: Add 200μL of 80% ethanol to...
steps.instruction: Add 200μL of 80% ethanol to the tubewhile in the magnetic rack

steps.title: Incubate at Room temperature for 30...
steps.instruction: Incubate at Room temperature for 30 seconds, and then carefully remove and discard the supernatant

steps.title: Repeat from Step 105
steps.instruction: Repeat from Step 105.8 once for a total of 2 washing steps

steps.title: Briefly spin the tubes and put...
steps.instruction: Briefly spin the tubes and put the tubes back on the magnetic rack

steps.title: Remove the residual ethanol pipetting and...
steps.instruction: Remove the residual ethanol pipetting and air dry the beads for up to 5 minutes while the tube is on the magnetic rack with lid open Do not over-dry the beads. Elute the samples when the beads are still dark brown and glossy looking, but when all visible liquid has evaporated. When the beads turn lighter brown and start to crack they are too dry

steps.title: Remove the tube from the magnetic rack
steps.instruction: Remove the tube from the magnetic rack

steps.title: Add 23μL of 0
steps.instruction: Add 23μL of 0.1X TE Buffer (provided) to elute the DNA from the beads

steps.title: Mix well on a vortex mixer
steps.instruction: Mix well on a vortex mixer

steps.title: Quickly spin the tube and incubate...
steps.instruction: Quickly spin the tube and incubate for 2 minutes at Room temperature

steps.title: Remove 20μL of the supernatant and...
steps.instruction: Remove 20μL of the supernatant and transfer to a clean 200µL nuclease free PCR tube without disturbing the beads pellet

steps.title: Determine concentration of DNA (Step 105
steps.instruction: Determine concentration of DNA (Step 105.17) using Qubit. Use 1 µL

steps.title: Store the DNA at -20°C or...
steps.instruction: Store the DNA at -20°C or proceed to the library quality assessment on Bioanalyzer DNA chip

steps.title: Allow all reagents to equilibrate to...
steps.instruction: Allow all reagents to equilibrate to room temperature for 30 minutes before use and protect dye and dye mixture from light

steps.title: Set up the priming station for...
steps.instruction: Set up the priming station for Bioanalyzer® chip adjusting the base plate in C position and sliding the syringe clip up to the bottom position Be sure of the clip position. Different position will lead to a negative output Preparing the Gel-Dye Mix

steps.title: Add 15 μL of High Sensitivity...
steps.instruction: Add 15 μL of High Sensitivity DNA dye concentrate (blue ) to a High Sensitivity DNA gel matrix vial (red )

steps.title: Vortex solution well and spin down
steps.instruction: Vortex solution well and spin down. Transfer to spin filter

steps.title: Centrifuge at 2240 g ± 20 % for 15 min
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 2240 g ± 20 % for 15 min. Store at 4 °C. Protect solution from light. Use prepared gel-dye mix within 6 weeks of preparation Chip loading Always insert the pipette tip to the bottom of the well when dispensing the liquid. Placing the pipette at the edge of the well may lead to poor results

steps.instruction: Pipette 1μL of sample in each of the 11 sample wells. Pipette 1μL of DNA Marker (green cap vial) in each unused sample well

steps.title: Positionate the chip horizontally in the...
steps.instruction: Positionate the chip horizontally in the IKA vortexer and vortex for 1 min at 2400rpm DO NOT VORTEX FOR MORE THAN 1 MINUTE AND MORE THAN 2400rpm

steps.title: Place the chip in the Agilent...
steps.instruction: Place the chip in the Agilent 2100 Bioanalyzer and run the instrument within 5 min Set up the Bioanalyzer instrument prior loading the chip. Keeping loaded gel for more than 5 minutes will allow the liquid in it to evaporate leading to a poor or non-acceptable analysis. For a clear overview of the DNA Average Fragment Length electropherogram output from the bioanalyzer see appendix 7 go to step #140

steps.title: Place all the reagents of the...
steps.instruction: Place all the reagents of the KAPA Library thawing on ice If the kit is used for the first time, add the Primer Premix (10X) (1 mL) to the bottle of KAPA SYBR® FAST qPCR Master Mix (2X) (5 mL). Mix thoroughly using a vortex mixer

steps.title: Prepare the appropriate library dilutions (using DNA dilution buffer)
steps.instruction: Prepare the appropriate library dilutions (using DNA dilution buffer). Depending on the expected concentration of the library, 1:1,000 – 1:100,000 dilutions may be appropriate All DNA Standards and library dilutions have to be assayed in triplicate. Each sample concentration should fall within the dynamic range of given standards.Check appendix 8 go to step #141 to see examples of dilution calculations

You can download it at the following link: https://www.google.it/url?sa=t&rct=j&q=&esrc=s&source=web&cd=1&ved=2ahUKEwjAyPrV2s_hAhWxlYsKHW-mB9gQFjAAegQIARAC&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.kapabiosystems.com%2Fdocument%2Fkapa-library-quantification-data-analysis-template%2F%3Fdl%3D1&usg=AOvVaw0cKEl4Os4HF1F8a

steps.instruction: Store the samples at -80°C

steps.instruction: Assemble the filtration system and place the 0.22 µm MF-membranes filters using RNase free forceps

steps.title: G2 test “G2 DNA/RNA Enhancer is...
steps.instruction: G2 test “G2 DNA/RNA Enhancer is convenient to use, when optimal DNA and or RNA extraction yield is required from especially clay. The primary function of G2 DNA/RNA Enhancer is to relieve inhibitory DNA-clay particle formations. G2 DNA/RNA Enhancer should be used in combination with either standardized extraction methods or commercial kits intended for DNA & RNA extraction from soil and clay” (Ampliqon). For this test were processed 500 mL and 1 mg of debris/soil for each sample with the addition of G2. According to protocol were added 2 mL of G2, to check the effect on the RNA extraction. A control without G2 was used. Graphic representation of the higher RNA yield using G2

steps.title: Relative Centrifugal Force ( RCF )...
steps.instruction: Relative Centrifugal Force ( RCF ) is the term used to describe the amount of accelerative force applied to a sample in a centrifuge. RCF is measured in multiples of the standard acceleration due to gravity at the Earth's surface (x g). This is why RCF and “x g ” are used interchangeably in centrifugation protocols. RCF or G = 1.12 x Radius (mm) x (rpm/1000) 2

steps.title: Preparing the RNA Ladder
steps.instruction: Preparing the RNA Ladder

steps.title: Spin the ladder down and pipette in an RNase-free vial
steps.instruction: Spin the ladder down and pipette in an RNase-free vial

steps.title: Heat denature the ladder for 2 min at 70 °C
steps.instruction: Heat denature the ladder for 2 min at 70 °C

steps.title: Immediately cool the vial on ice
steps.instruction: Immediately cool the vial on ice

steps.title: Add 90μL of RNase-free water and mix thoroughly
steps.instruction: Add 90μL of RNase-free water and mix thoroughly

steps.title: Prepare aliquots in recommended 0
steps.instruction: Prepare aliquots in recommended 0.5 mL RNase-free vials with the required amount for typical daily use

steps.title: Store aliquots at -80 °C
steps.instruction: Store aliquots at -80 °C . After initial heat denaturation, the frozen aliquots should not require repeated heat denaturation

steps.title: Before use, thaw ladder aliquots on ice Avoid extensive warming
steps.instruction: Before use, thaw ladder aliquots on ice Avoid extensive warming

steps.title: Centrifuge at 1500g ± 20 %...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 1500g ± 20 % for 10 min at Room temperature

steps.title: Aliquot 65μL filtered gel into 0
steps.instruction: Aliquot 65μL filtered gel into 0.5 mL RNase-free microcentrifuge tubes.Use filtered gel within 4 weeks. Store at 4 °C .

steps.title: In the following section can be...
steps.instruction: In the following section can be understood how the bioanalyzer works and the regions evaluated to check the final quality score Electropherogram detailing the regions that are indicative of RNA quality Sample electropherograms used to assess the RNA Integrity Number (RIN) software. Samples range from intact (RIN 10), to degraded (RIN 2)

steps.title: Examples of how to perform PCR...
steps.instruction: Examples of how to perform PCR enrichment (Indexing) with 4 samples: The only difference in the mixture is the Indexing choice ( Highlighted in Bold ). NebNext Multiplex Oligoes for Illumina (Index Primers Set 1) contains 12 index primers and they can all be used following the previous example for 12 samples.

steps.title: Assessment of DNA library fragment length...
steps.instruction: Assessment of DNA library fragment length average Figure showing an example of correct RNA library size distribution on a Bioanalyzer. The electropherogram should show a narrow distribution with a peak size approximately 300 bp. If a peak at ~ 80 bp (primers) or 128 bp (adaptor-dimer) is visible in the Bioanalyzer traces; Bring up the sample volume (from Step 104.18) to 50 μl with 0.1X TE buffer and repeat the SPRIselect Bead or NEBNext Sample Purification Bead Cleanup Step (Section 105.1). A dilution may be necessary for running on a Bioanalyzer High Sensitivity DNA Chip

steps.title: qPCR dilutions calculation Prepare the appropriate...
steps.instruction: qPCR dilutions calculation Prepare the appropriate library dilutions (using DNA dilution buffer). Depending on the expected concentration of the library, 1:1,000 – 1:100,000 dilutions may be appropriate. It is recommended to perform a ten-fold serial dilution to obtain a more reliable dilution. Follow the empiric calculation: 1 ng/µL from the library with a 300 bp length it’s equal to say 3.5 nM in concentration So, 1ng/µL = 3.5 nM in concentration Practical calculation: If we have a sample with 20 ng/µL from the library, multiply 20 ng/µL for 3.5 (conversion value) : 20 ng/µL x 3.5 = 70 nM The value in µM has to be transformed in pM and diluted a proper number of times to fall in the following standard table provided by the KAPABiosynthesis. 70 nM = 70.000 pM Possibly we should dilute the samples to fall between standard 3 and 5. 70.000 pM has to be diluted at least 100.000 times in order to obtain a final concentration of 0.7 pM. In this case an additional 10-fold dilution of each sample is recommended.
